meta.description: Spinal surgical rod, rail, transition rod and transition rail reference data for the Leeds Spinal Planner
meta.notes: All dimensions in millimetres. Sources include manufacturer product catalogues, FDA 510(k) submissions, and peer-reviewed biomechanical literature.

rod.label: Rod
rod.description: Standard circular cross-section rod. The vast majority of posterior spinal fixation uses round rods.

rail.label: Rail
rail.description: Rectangular or non-circular cross-section rod. Provides greater stiffness per unit size than equivalent round rod. The K2M MESA Rail is the primary commercial example (5.5mm CoCr rail is 210% stronger in flex-ext and 46% stronger in lateral bending than a 5.5mm CoCr round rod).

products.name: K2M MESA Rail
products.note: Originally K2M, acquired by Stryker 2018, now part of VB Spine (Apr 2025). The only widely used rectangular-profile spinal fixation rod.

transitionRod.label: Transition Rod
transitionRod.description: A single rod that tapers from one circular diameter to another along its length, bridging different spinal regions (e.g., 3.5mm cervical tapering to 5.5mm thoracolumbar). Eliminates the need for a domino connector at the diameter change point.

transitionRail.label: Transition Rail
transitionRail.description: A single rail that transitions from one cross-sectional size to another along its length. As with standard rails, uses a rectangular or non-circular profile. Less common than transition rods; most diameter transitions use round rods or domino connectors.
transitionRail.note: No widely documented commercial examples as of Feb 2026. The K2M MESA Rail system uses domino connectors rather than tapered rails for diameter transitions. Included here as a logical category for completeness and future products.

diameters.usage: Standard thoracolumbar -- the most widely used diameter
High-stiffness thoracolumbar fixation (210% stronger in flex-ext than equivalent round rod)
Standard thoracolumbar (stiffer). Often paired with 5.5mm in dual-diameter screw systems

transitionCombinations.application: Cervicothoracic junction -- the most common transition

products.name: Stryker OASYS Rod-to-Rod Connector
products.note: Connector-based transition, not a tapered rod

products.name: DePuy Synthes EXPEDIUM Wedding Band Connector
Spinal Resources Bezier Transition Rod
products.note: Smooth Bezier curve taper through 6.0-5.5-5.0mm. Patient-specific. FDA-cleared.

products.name: Spinal Resources Bezier Transition Rod
products.note: Smooth Bezier curve taper through 5.5-5.0-4.75mm. 16% stress reduction on upper adjacent vertebrae vs constant 6.0mm rod.

materials.name: Titanium Alloy
materials.notes: Best biocompatibility, closest modulus to cortical bone, excellent corrosion resistance. The default material for most spinal rods.

materials.name: Commercially Pure Titanium
materials.notes: Softer and more ductile than Ti-6Al-4V. Used by Globus/NuVasive Reline and Medtronic Solera.

materials.name: Cobalt-Chromium-Molybdenum
materials.notes: Highest stiffness and strength. Best fatigue resistance (4.5x titanium). 5.5mm CoCr is approximately equivalent in stiffness to 6.0mm Ti. Lowest rod fracture rate. Trade names: Medtronic CHROMALOY / CHROMALOY Plus, Stryker Vitallium.
materials.availability: Medtronic, DePuy Synthes, Globus Medical, Stryker, Orthofix, K2M MESA, Zimmer Biomet

materials.name: Stainless Steel
materials.notes: Long track record, cheapest. Greatest MRI artefact, nickel allergy concern, heaviest. Largely superseded by CoCr and Ti in modern systems. Still used in some deformity systems and developing markets.

materials.name: PEEK
materials.notes: Closest modulus to bone. Semi-rigid fixation -- cannot provide rigid fixation. Cannot be bent intraoperatively. May reduce adjacent segment degeneration. Available in round (5.5, 6.0, 6.35mm) and oval (6.35x7.2mm) profiles.
materials.availability: Medtronic CD Horizon PEEK, DePuy Synthes EXPEDIUM/VIPER PEEK, Globus TRANSITION

materials.name: Nitinol
materials.notes: Shape memory effect: cold-straightened rods revert to pre-programmed curve when warmed. Superelastic. Used in scoliosis correction research. Less operative time, less blood loss, improved coronal correction vs traditional rods in scoliosis studies. Not widely commercially available in mainstream posterior systems.

lengths.description: Stock straight lengths sold by manufacturers. Rods are cut to size intraoperatively.

mis.label: MIS / Percutaneous
mis.note: Pre-cut for single/two-level minimally invasive constructs. May have specialised end shapes for tube insertion.

open.label: Open Surgery
open.note: Most common starting lengths for long constructs: 500mm (33% of cases) and 600mm (22%). Cut to final length intraoperatively.

contouringOptions.label: Straight
contouringOptions.description: Stock straight rod. Surgeon contours manually intraoperatively using French benders and in-situ benders.

contouringOptions.label: Pre-Lordosed
contouringOptions.description: Factory-bent with a lordotic curve. Available in set angles (e.g., 10, 20, 30, 40 degrees). Reduces intraoperative bending time.

contouringOptions.label: Pre-Kyphosed
contouringOptions.description: Factory-bent with a kyphotic curve for thoracic constructs.

contouringOptions.label: Patient-Specific Pre-Contoured
contouringOptions.description: 3D-printed or CNC-machined to patient's planned sagittal and coronal contour from preoperative CT. Superior immediate sagittal alignment vs surgeon-contoured rods. Reduced proximal junctional failure in adult spinal deformity.

connectors.description: Devices connecting two rods or rails, including cross-region diameter transitions

types.label: Parallel (Side-by-Side)
types.description: Two rods sit parallel, clamped together. Used for extension of existing constructs and revision surgery.

types.label: Axial (End-to-End / In-Line)
types.description: Two rod ends meet in line. Used for longitudinal extension and growing rods.

types.label: Wedding Band
types.description: Ring-style connector around parallel rods. Used for growing rod lengthening procedures.

types.label: Offset
types.description: Rods connected with a lateral offset. Used when rod paths do not align.

types.label: Cross-Connector (Transverse)
types.description: Links two parallel longitudinal rods across the midline. One cross-connector adds ~45% torsional stiffness; two add ~63%. Primarily benefits axial rotation resistance. Typically compatible with 5.5mm and 6.0mm systems.

dominoCombinations.source: Zimmer Biomet Polaris, Stryker OASYS, Orthofix CENTURION
Zimmer Biomet Polaris, DePuy Synthes EXPEDIUM, most systems
Zimmer Biomet Polaris, DePuy Synthes EXPEDIUM

systems.name: QUARTEX
systems.note: Extensive connector/adapter portfolio for revision and complex cervicothoracic constructs

systems.name: OASYS
systems.note: Includes 3.5-5.5mm transition rods and rod-to-rod connectors for 3.5 to 3.5/4.5/5.5/6.0mm

manufacturerSystems.note: Stryker sold U.S. spinal implants to VB Spine (Apr 2025). Products now distributed by VB Spine.

systems.name: K2M MESA Rail
systems.note: Rectangular cross-section. 210% stronger in flex-ext, 46% stronger in lateral bending vs equivalent round CoCr rod.

manufacturerSystems.note: VB Spine (Viscogliosi Brothers) acquired Stryker's U.S. spine business including K2M products (Apr 2025).

systems.name: Polaris
systems.note: Widest documented domino connector range (3.5-6.35mm in all combinations)

systems.name: Phoenix MIS
systems.note: Hex-end rods for anti-rotation during percutaneous insertion (body is still round)

systems.name: CENTURION POCT
systems.note: Occipitocervicothoracic system with 3.5-5.5mm transition rods and cross-connectors

clinicalNotes: 5.5mm CoCr rod is approximately equivalent in stiffness to 6.0mm Ti rod -- a thinner CoCr rod can match or exceed the rigidity of a larger Ti rod.
Dual-diameter screws accepting both 5.5mm and 6.0mm rods are increasingly common, giving surgeons intraoperative flexibility.
Rod fracture is the most common mode of construct failure. CoCr has significantly lower fracture rates than Ti, especially in long adult deformity constructs.
MIS/percutaneous rods are pre-cut to shorter lengths (30-150mm) and may have specialised end shapes for insertion through tubes.
A 5.5mm CoCr MESA Rail is 210% stronger in flex-ext and 46% stronger in lateral bending than a 5.5mm CoCr round rod.
One transverse cross-connector adds ~45% torsional stiffness; two add ~63%.
Bezier parametric curve transition rods (Spinal Resources) reduce contact pressures by up to 90% at the transition midpoint compared to constant-diameter rods.
PEEK rods cannot be bent intraoperatively and provide semi-rigid fixation only. They may reduce adjacent segment degeneration.

The rail vs rod distinction is important for record keeping: a rail has a rectangular cross-section providing directional stiffness, while a rod has a circular cross-section providing uniform stiffness in all planes.
